Nectar Farms.price_notes: Price not publicly disclosed. Structure uses Neoen's 20 MW / 34 MWh Tesla battery to firm supply to Nectar Farms' glasshouse.
Nectar Farms.tenor_description: 10-year PPA signed 2018. Bulgana achieved full grid connection in 2021; operations ramped through 2020–2021.

sources.title: Neoen to break ground on Bulgana Green Power Hub — Neoen
Bulgana Green Power Hub — Victorian Department of Energy
Bulgana Green Power Hub, Wimmera, Victoria — NS Energy

AEMO (system services).volume_structure: 250 MW of 300 MW / 450 MWh battery reserved for AEMO SIPS during summer months; Neoen retains 50 MW in summer and full 300 MW outside summer for market trading.
AEMO (system services).price_notes: Annual SIPS service fee not publicly disclosed. Availability payment, not $/MWh. Recovered from Victorian transmission customers via AEMO.
AEMO (system services).tenor_description: SIPS contract runs from commissioning (December 2021) until 2032 (~11 years).

sources.title: AEMO — Victorian battery ready to deliver energy reserves this summer
Background on Victorian Big Battery and SIPS — WattClarity

NSW Government (EII).volume_structure: ~700 MW of 850 MW / 1680 MWh battery reserved for SIPS shock-absorber service (balance available for market trading).
NSW Government (EII).price_notes: Specific contract value not publicly disclosed. AER revenue determination governs cost recovery. Not an LTESA; procured outside standard NSW LTESA tender.
NSW Government (EII).tenor_description: 5.5-year SIPS contract. Partial commercial operation from ~August 2025; full operation expected in 2026.

sources.title: AER — Waratah Super Battery SIPS battery service
EnergyCo — Waratah Super Battery Project

SA Government.volume_structure: No SA Government offtake contract disclosed; AGL merchant/self-dispatched.
SA Government.price_notes: No disclosed SA Government PPA or tolling agreement. SA Government support limited to planning fast-tracking. AEMO did not conclude a SIPS-style contract with SA batteries.

sources.title: AGL opens 250 MW grid-scale battery at Torrens Island — AGL

ActewAGL.developer: Goyder Wind Farm 1A Pty Ltd as trustee for Goyder Wind Farm 1A Trust
ActewAGL.price_notes: No ActewAGL PPA disclosed. ActewAGL acts as retail delivery entity under ACT FIT framework. May be mis-attributed from ACT Government.

sources.title: Goyder South — Goyder Renewables Zone

ActewAGL.developer: Goyder Wind Farm 1B Pty Ltd as trustee for Goyder Wind Farm 1B Trust
ActewAGL.price_notes: No ActewAGL PPA publicly disclosed for Goyder 1B. Known 1B offtake: BHP 70 MW baseload (2022).

sources.title: BHP — New wind and battery project in South Australia

Alinta Energy.volume_structure: Alinta Energy offtake not confirmed in public sources; only reported long-term PPA is Snowy Hydro for 75% of output over 15 years
Alinta Energy.price_notes: No Alinta-Ryan Corner PPA found publicly; confirmed PPA is Snowy Hydro (15-yr CfD, 75%).

sources.title: RenewEconomy — Snowy Hydro deal paves way for 218MW Ryan Corner wind farm
Ryan Corner Wind Farm — Project page (Global Power Generation)

Victorian Government.volume_structure: Output from 29 of 80 turbines (~37% of project output; ~126 MW equivalent)
Victorian Government.price_notes: Individual project strike price NOT publicly disclosed (commercial-in-confidence). VRET1 wind weighted reference price reported as ~$56.52/MWh; individual winning bids not released.

sources.title: Tilt Renewables successful in VRET — Tilt Renewables
VRET1 — energy.vic.gov.au

ALDI.volume_structure: Approximately 6% of project output (~20 MW equivalent of 336 MW project)
ALDI.price_notes: Price not publicly disclosed. Tilt described pricing as reflecting tightening Victorian wind PPA market at time of signing (April 2020).
ALDI.tenor_description: 10-year PPA signed April 2020; offtake commenced at the beginning of 2021.

sources.title: Tilt Renewables and ALDI Sign a Long Term PPA from the Dundonnell Wind Farm

ALDI signs decade-long PPA for wind farm output — Energy Magazine

Woolworths.developer: BWF Nominees Pty Ltd as trustee for the BWF Trust
Woolworths.volume_structure: 195,000 MWh/year (~60 MW equivalent of 244 MW project). Covers 30% of Woolworths NSW demand including 108 supermarkets.
Woolworths.tenor_description: 10-year PPA signed June 2021. Offtake commenced January 2022; Bango fully operational 2023.

sources.title: Woolworths Group invests in PPA with NSW wind farm — Woolworths
Woolworths Group invests in PPA with CWP Renewables — Squadron Energy
Ashurst advises Woolworths on PPA for Bango 999 Wind Farm — Ashurst

Woolworths.project_name: Port Augusta Renewable Energy Park - Wind
Woolworths.volume_structure: ~100,000 MWh/year (~25 MW equivalent) from 210 MW wind component of hybrid. Covers 100% of Woolworths' SA load (70 supermarkets + 17 Big W + DC).
Woolworths.tenor_description: 9-year PPA; park completed 2022 (initially targeted 2021). Woolworths' SA operations fully supplied by Port Augusta from 2022 onward.

sources.title: Woolworths signs supply deal with Australia's biggest wind-solar energy park — Energy Matters
Woolworths inks PPA to reach 100% renewable in South Australia — Reccessary

Flow Power.volume_structure: 40 MW fixed-capacity offtake out of 209 MW Goyder 1A (part of 412 MW Stage 1)

Zen Energy.volume_structure: 200 GWh/year (~34% of Waubra output, ~65 MW equivalent of 192 MW project)
Zen Energy.price_notes: Price not publicly disclosed. Second 10-year tranche signed 2024 extending offtake to end 2033 at revised terms.
Zen Energy.tenor_description: 10-year PPA signed September 2022. Second 10-year offtake commenced January 2024, running to at least December 2033. Waubra operational since 2009.

Stanwell Corporation.developer: Macintyre Windfarm Pty Ltd and Arc Energy Macintyre Pty Ltd
Stanwell Corporation.volume_structure: Approximately 150 MW under PPA (remainder of the 550 MW combined state-owned offtake after CleanCo's 400 MW). Exact figure not publicly broken out.
Stanwell Corporation.tenor_description: Long-term PPA; wind farm started generating October 2024

sources.title: Acciona Energia's MacIntyre Wind Farm starts generating electricity
MacIntyre Wind Farm powers up in Queensland - Acciona, Stanwell, CleanCo

Stanwell Corporation.volume_structure: 348 MW (approx 75% of Stage 1 450 MW, or approx 31% of full 1,135 MW project) under PPA
Stanwell Corporation.tenor_description: 15-year PPA signed August 2020; first turbines installed 2023, energy flowing from 2023

sources.title: Massive Clarke Creek wind farm inks deal with Queensland coal generator
Queensland inks 348 MW offtake for Clarke Creek
PPA signed for part of 800 MW Clarke Creek wind project in Queensland

Stanwell Corporation.developer: Cubico Sustainable Investments / Stanwell Corporation (50:50 JV)
Stanwell Corporation.volume_structure: 50% of 506 MW output (approx 253 MW) under 15-year PPA from Cubico's share, plus 50% equity - effectively 100% of output accrues to Stanwell
Stanwell Corporation.price_notes: Price not publicly disclosed; backed by A$455m Queensland Renewable Energy and Hydrogen Jobs Fund contribution to Stanwell's equity
Stanwell Corporation.tenor_description: 15-year PPA for Cubico's 50%; Stage 1 (252 MW) turbines powered up 2025, Stage 2 (254 MW) scheduled for 2026

sources.title: Wambo Wind Farm - Stanwell
Wind farm doubles in size to deliver clean power to equivalent of 312,000 homes
Wambo Wind Farm 50% ownership to propel renewable energy transformation

Equinix.developer: Golden Plains WF1 Pty Ltd as trustee for the Golden Plains WF1 Unit Trust
Equinix.volume_structure: 151 MW (~20% of 756 MW stage one); covers full load of 17 Australian IBX data centres
Equinix.tenor_description: Long-term PPA signed February 2024. Supply commences once stage one operational (expected 2029 per TagEnergy). Ashurst advised Equinix.

sources.title: Equinix Signs its First Renewable Energy PPA in Australia with TagEnergy
TagEnergy signs second PPA for stage one of Golden Plains — TagEnergy
Massive Golden Plains wind farm snags a second PPA — RenewEconomy

Iberdrola portfolio.developer: Flyers Creek Wind Farm Pty Ltd
Iberdrola portfolio.volume_structure: Full 145 MW nameplate (~450 GWh/year) dispatched into Iberdrola Australia NEM portfolio; no publicly named project-specific corporate PPA.
Iberdrola portfolio.price_structure: Portfolio dispatch — no disclosed single offtake PPA
Iberdrola portfolio.price_notes: No publicly disclosed project-level PPA price or counterparty. Output is merchant + portfolio-firmed sales including Iberdrola's PPA book (BHP, Woolworths, others).
Iberdrola portfolio.tenor_description: Iberdrola committed to construction February 2022. First power to grid 2024. Portfolio-dispatched — no discrete tenor.

sources.title: Flyers Creek Wind Farm — Iberdrola Australia
Iberdrola to construct 145MW Australian onshore wind farm — reNews
New NSW wind farm sends first bursts of power to the grid — RenewEconomy

Neoen portfolio.volume_structure: 100% of output (157 MW / 457 GWh/year) to CleanCo Queensland
Neoen portfolio.price_notes: Price not publicly disclosed. Supported by A$370M Neoen investment including NAIF financing component.
Neoen portfolio.tenor_description: 15-year PPA signed October 2020. Kaban Green Power Hub connected to Queensland's transmission network and started powering up in 2023.

sources.title: Neoen and CleanCo Queensland sign a new PPA for 110 MW — Neoen
Kaban Green Power Hub — CleanCo Queensland
Kaban wind farm powers up — RenewEconomy

Ark Energy/Telstra.developer: Macintyre Windfarm Pty Ltd and Arc Energy Macintyre Pty Ltd
Ark Energy/Telstra.volume_structure: 350 GWh/year to Telstra (~100 MW equivalent of 923 MW project). Remainder of Ark Energy's 30% stake (~177 MW) supplies Sun Metals zinc refinery.
Ark Energy/Telstra.price_notes: Price not publicly disclosed. Described as Australia's largest corporate wind PPA by volume at announcement.
Ark Energy/Telstra.tenor_description: Long-term PPA signed October 2022. Energy supply commenced as MacIntyre ramped up through 2024; full commissioning 2025.
